insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change enables more flexibility in querying data specific to different sales channels. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The GraphQL query should include the @inContext directive with the channelId argument, like @inContext(channelId: 'channel-id')
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales channel-specific data in their storefronts, enhancing their overall shopping experience.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and accuracy in querying sales channel-specific data, which is crucial for merchants with multiple sales channels.

also_worth_knowing.text: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics

updates.title: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
updates.summary: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various resources and metafield changes.

updates.title: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
updates.summary: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building.

updates.title: Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics
updates.summary: Full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, making it easier for app developers to build merchant-facing analytics.

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available, providing early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirements have been updated to include using a unique name for apps, reducing merchant confusion over identical names.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId, allowing apps to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields, providing more information about exchange line items.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
CLI version 4.5.1 bumps cli-hydrogen to 13.0.1, fixing issues and improving stability.

recap.headline: Enhanced Events, Liquid Templates, and Inventory APIs

themes.title: Event Subscriptions
themes.detail: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various resources such as Order, Collection, and InventoryItem. This update also includes metafield changes on Product, Order, Customer, Collection, and Location.

themes.title: Theme Development
themes.detail: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, introducing a simpler way to build theme pages. Additionally, the CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2, fixing warnings and bumping Shopify/theme-tools packages.

themes.title: Inventory and Analytics
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview provides early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API. Furthermore, full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, simplifying the process of building merchant-facing analytics.

actions.text: Update apps to utilize new event subscriptions
Explore the physical inventory feature preview
